data.title: As a re-sync process, I can read a Figma design and convert it to spatial-layout.json format
data.description:
## Description

**What**: REST API GET /v1/files/:key retrieves the Figma JSON node tree. A mapping layer converts Figma node types to spatial-layout.json schema fields with a defined component identity matching strategy for reconciliation with the original manifest.
**Why**: Transforms Figma's raw design data into DSO's structured format, enabling the merge algorithm to compare original and revised designs at the component level.
**Scope**:
- IN: REST API GET /v1/files/:key call, depth-limited traversal for large files, Figma node type mapping (FRAME→Section, TEXT→TextNode, RECTANGLE→Shape, COMPONENT→Component, INSTANCE→Instance), absoluteBoundingBox→spatial_hint, fills/strokes→props, characters→text content, component identity matching (Figma node name to component id, structural position fallback), output as valid spatial-layout.json
- OUT: Behavioral merge (S3), re-sync orchestration (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, GET /v1/files/:key retrieves the full Figma JSON node tree with depth-limited traversal to prevent OOM on large files
  ← Satisfies: "reads the revised design from Figma via REST API"
- When this story is complete, Figma node types are mapped to spatial-layout.json schema: FRAME/GROUP→hierarchy, absoluteBoundingBox→spatial_hint, fills/strokes/effects→props, characters→text content, layoutMode→responsive hints
  ← Satisfies: "maps Figma JSON node properties to spatial-layout.json schema fields"
- When this story is complete, each Figma node is matched to its original spatial-layout.json component using a defined identity strategy (match by node name to component id, structural position fallback for unnamed nodes); unmatched Figma nodes flagged as designer-added, unmatched originals flagged as designer-removed; matching exposed as a separate function for S3
  ← Satisfies: adversarial review finding on identity matching contract
- When this story is complete, the output is a valid spatial-layout.json that passes the existing schema validation
  ← Satisfies: "regenerates the complete 3-artifact design manifest"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] REST API rate limits 10-20/min for Dev/Full seats; depth-limit traversal for large files
- [Testing] Figma JSON structure varies by node type — comprehensive mapping tests for FRAME, TEXT, RECTANGLE, COMPONENT, INSTANCE, GROUP, VECTOR at minimum

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
